# AI Transparency Statement

_UK GDPR required document template. Replace all [placeholders] with your organisation details before publication._

**Source:** `External - Customer-Facing Documents/AI Transparency Statement.docx`

---

AI Transparency Statement

## Uk Gdpr

Overview

What AI does in [Product Name]

[Product Name] uses Microsoft Azure OpenAI (UK South) for optional features including note summarisation, speech-to-text (Whisper), entity suggestions, AI-assisted editing commands, and search ranking.

[Product Name] is a workspace and productivity platform. It is not a medical device, clinical system, or regulated health record. Users must not rely on [Product Name] for diagnosis, treatment, or clinical decision-making. AI outputs are assistive drafts only.

AI does not make automated decisions with legal or similarly significant effects. Outputs are drafts for human review.

Data handling

What is sent to AI

Content required for the feature (e.g. note body, audio chunks) — scoped to your organisation (tenant)

System instructions fixed by [Company Name] — not editable by customers

No cross-tenant data in any prompt

What is not stored

Prompts and raw completions are not written to databases or logs

Only structured outputs you explicitly save (e.g. summary text, transcript) are persisted

Telemetry records feature name, token counts, latency — not content

Your choices

Control

AI features require user action (except background entity enrichment clearly disclosed)

Organisation admins may disable AI features where contract tier allows

Report concerns to privacy@[company-domain]

_Template — customize confidentiality and ownership statements for your organisation before distribution._
